children.name: Overnight Oats — Berry Boost Variation
children.short_description: Creamy overnight oats with apple and cinnamon—a protein- and fiber-rich breakfast.
children.meal_suggestion: Mix ingredients to make overnight oats: rolled oats, milk, plain yogurt, diced apple, and cinnamon. Refrigerate overnight and enjoy cold or warmed.
children.nutrition_note: A balanced, protein- and fiber-rich breakfast. Oats provide soluble fiber; yogurt adds protein and calcium; apple contributes vitamins and extra fiber. Cinnamon adds flavor with minimal calories and may help with blood sugar regulation.

children.name: Smoothie — Lemon-Herb Style
children.short_description: Creamy banana-milk smoothie with peanut butter and oats; balanced protein, carbs, and fats with fiber.
children.meal_suggestion: Creamy, protein-forward smoothie great for breakfast or post-workout. For a lemon-herb twist, blend in lemon zest and a few fresh herbs (mint or basil) with the listed ingredients.
children.nutrition_note: Approx. 550–600 kcal per serving. Protein ~22–28 g; carbs ~60–70 g; fat ~22–28 g. Rich in potassium (banana), calcium/vitamin D (milk), and fiber (oats). Note: the lemon-herb flavor is not represented by the current ingredients; to add lemon/herb notes, blend in lemon zest and fresh herbs (e.g., mint or basil) — this will adjust macros slightly.

children.name: Granola & Milk — Garlic-Ginger Plate
children.short_description: A quick bowl combining granola, milk, and banana for a simple breakfast or snack.
children.meal_suggestion: Keep as 1 serving granola + 1 cup milk + 1 medium banana. For more protein, use higher-protein milk or add yogurt; to reduce sugar, choose unsweetened granola. If you want a savory nod to the name, add a tiny pinch of minced garlic and grated ginger, but use sparingly as this will alter flavor and nutrition.
children.nutrition_note: Carbs from granola and banana; protein and calcium from milk. May be high in added sugars if granola is sweetened; consider unsweetened granola or smaller portions and add fiber with berries or seeds.

children.name: Caprese Sandwich — Maple-Kissed Variation
children.short_description: Caprese-inspired sandwich on whole-grain bread with mozzarella, tomato, basil, and balsamic; maple-kissed variation optional with a light maple glaze.
children.meal_suggestion: A quick, balanced lunch: Caprese sandwich on whole-grain bread with mozzarella, tomato, basil, and balsamic. For the maple-kissed twist, drizzle a small amount of maple-balsamic glaze.
children.nutrition_note: Includes fiber from whole-grain bread and protein plus calcium from mozzarella; tomatoes and basil supply vitamins and antioxidants; balsamic adds flavor with modest sugars. A maple-kissed variation can use a light maple-balsamic glaze to introduce sweetness without heavy sugar load.

children.name: Lentil Soup — Citrus Fresh Set
children.short_description: A hearty, plant-based lentil soup with vegetables served alongside whole-grain bread—protein- and fiber-rich for a vegetarian lunch or dinner.
children.meal_suggestion: Serve hot with warm whole-grain bread; brighten with a squeeze of lemon or lemon zest to echo the Citrus Fresh theme.
children.nutrition_note: Plant-based protein and fiber from lentils, plus vitamins/minerals from carrots, celery, and onion. Whole-grain bread adds extra fiber and complex carbs. For lower sodium, use a low-sodium broth; a squeeze of lemon can boost citrus brightness.

children.name: Chicken Noodle Soup — Citrus Fresh Twist
children.short_description: A comforting chicken noodle soup with carrots and celery in broth, offering protein, vegetables, and warmth with a suggested citrus brightness.
children.meal_suggestion: Finish with lemon zest or juice to emphasize the citrus twist; for more fiber, use whole-grain noodles and pair with a small fruit serving for extra vitamin C.
children.nutrition_note: Protein from chicken and carbohydrates from noodles provide balanced nutrition; carrots and celery add fiber and micronutrients; using a light broth helps keep fat moderate. The citrus twist is not shown in the listed ingredients, so lemon would boost vitamin C if added.

children.name: Creamy Tomato Soup with Grilled Cheese Dippers
children.short_description: A kid-friendly classic — velvety tomato soup served with crispy whole-wheat grilled cheese strips for dunking.
children.meal_suggestion: Sauté onion and garlic in olive oil, add canned crushed tomatoes and a splash of milk or cream, then blend until smooth. Season with basil, salt, and pepper. Serve in a bowl alongside whole-wheat grilled cheese cut into sticks for easy dipping. A drizzle of olive oil on top adds richness.
children.nutrition_note: Tomatoes are rich in lycopene and vitamin C; whole-wheat bread provides fiber and complex carbs; cheddar adds calcium and protein. Milk contributes additional calcium. Keep cream or milk portions moderate to manage fat; using part-skim cheese or reduced-fat milk can lighten the dish while preserving flavor.

children.name: Teriyaki Chicken Rice Bowl
children.short_description: Sweet and savory teriyaki-glazed chicken over steamed rice with steamed broccoli and shredded carrots — colorful, balanced, and easy to eat.
children.meal_suggestion: Pan-sear bite-sized chicken pieces until golden, then toss in a homemade teriyaki glaze (soy sauce, honey, garlic, ginger). Serve over steamed rice with steamed broccoli florets and shredded carrots on the side. Garnish with sesame seeds and sliced scallions for a restaurant-style touch.
children.nutrition_note: Chicken breast provides lean protein; broccoli is rich in vitamin C, vitamin K, and fiber; carrots add beta-carotene. Rice supplies energy from carbohydrates. Homemade teriyaki lets you control sugar and sodium — use low-sodium soy sauce and moderate honey to keep it balanced.

children.name: Mini Meatball Pasta Bake
children.short_description: Tender turkey meatballs baked with whole-wheat penne in marinara sauce and topped with melted mozzarella — hearty comfort food with a nutritious twist.
children.meal_suggestion: Mix ground turkey with breadcrumbs, egg, garlic, and Italian seasoning; roll into small meatballs and bake at 400°F for 15 minutes. Toss cooked whole-wheat penne with marinara sauce and the meatballs in a baking dish, top with shredded mozzarella, and bake 10 more minutes until bubbly. Serve with a side of steamed green beans or a small salad.
children.nutrition_note: Ground turkey is a leaner alternative to beef, providing protein with less saturated fat; whole-wheat pasta adds fiber for sustained energy; marinara sauce supplies lycopene from tomatoes. Mozzarella provides calcium. Watch portion sizes of cheese and pasta to manage calories; pair with vegetables for a more complete meal.

context_trace.description: Matched agg_exists(Meal[atom(content =~ "portable caprese sandwich")]) on Day 2 (Score: 1.00)

children.name: Granola & Milk — Garden Veg Bowl
children.short_description: A quick granola, milk, and banana bowl offering energy, calcium, potassium, and fiber; customizable with higher-protein milk or added vegetables/fruits.
children.meal_suggestion: To boost protein, use higher-protein milk or add Greek yogurt. For more vegetables, top with a handful of spinach or berries to enhance fiber and micronutrients.
children.nutrition_note: Carb-rich bowl with protein and calcium from milk; banana adds potassium and fiber. Monitor added sugars in granola; choose unsweetened or lower-sugar varieties and watch portion size.

children.name: Avocado Toast — Smoky Skillet
children.short_description: Whole-grain avocado toast with lemon and egg, smoky skillet-inspired.
children.meal_suggestion: Toast the bread; mash avocado with a squeeze of lemon; spread on toast; top with a fried or poached egg. Optional: a pinch of smoked paprika to add smoky flavor.
children.nutrition_note: Balanced mix: complex carbs and fiber from whole-grain bread; healthy fats from avocado; high-quality protein from egg; lemon adds vitamin C. Helps with satiety and steady energy. Contains gluten and eggs.

children.name: Smoothie — Berry Boost Skillet
children.short_description: Creamy banana-peanut butter oat smoothie with balanced carbs, protein, and fats.
children.meal_suggestion: Blend banana, milk, peanut butter, and oats until smooth for a creamy drink; add ice for a cooler consistency. Optional toppings: sliced banana, berries, or a sprinkle of extra oats.
children.nutrition_note: Protein- and fiber-rich smoothie from banana, oats, peanut butter, and milk; provides potassium and healthy fats. Use unsweetened milk to limit sugar, and add berries for extra antioxidants if desired.

children.name: Caprese Sandwich — Citrus Fresh Variation
children.short_description: Caprese-style sandwich on whole-grain bread with mozzarella, tomato, basil, and balsamic—a fresh, protein- and fiber-rich lunch option.
children.meal_suggestion: One sandwich serves as a balanced lunch. For the Citrus Fresh Variation, add a light squeeze of lemon or orange juice over the filling or serve with citrus wedges; consider a small side salad for extra greens.
children.nutrition_note: Fiber from whole-grain bread and vegetables; protein from mozzarella; vitamins and antioxidants from tomato and basil; balsamic adds flavor with modest calories.

children.name: Teriyaki Chicken Bowl — Garden Veg Style
children.short_description: Teriyaki chicken bowl with garden vegetables over rice — a protein-rich, veggie-forward meal.
children.meal_suggestion: A quick, balanced bowl of lean chicken, rice, and mixed vegetables coated in teriyaki glaze. For a lighter version, swap in brown rice and low-sodium sauce to increase fiber and lower sodium.
children.nutrition_note: Protein from chicken; carbohydrates from rice; fiber and micronutrients from mixed vegetables; teriyaki sauce adds flavor but can be high in sodium; to boost fiber and reduce sodium, use brown rice and/or a reduced-sodium sauce and add more vegetables as desired.

children.name: Caprese Sandwich — Maple-Kissed Set
children.short_description: Caprese-inspired sandwich on whole-grain bread with mozzarella, tomato, basil, and balsamic.
children.meal_suggestion: Enjoy with a side salad or fruit; drizzle a teaspoon of olive oil for healthy fats if desired; keep portion size moderate to fit a balanced meal.
children.nutrition_note: Balanced meal with protein from mozzarella and fiber from whole-grain bread; tomato and basil provide vitamins and antioxidants; balsamic adds flavor with minimal calories; moderate sodium from cheese. For lower fat, use part-skim mozzarella.

children.name: Beef & Veggie Stew — Mild-Spice Bake
children.short_description: Beef and veggie stew inspired by a mild-spice bake, featuring tender stew beef with potatoes, carrots, and celery simmered in savory broth.
children.meal_suggestion: Serve hot in bowls with a side of steamed greens or a simple salad, and optional crusty whole-grain bread. Garnish with fresh herbs (e.g., parsley) for added flavor without extra calories.
children.nutrition_note: Protein-rich beef provides essential amino acids; potatoes add energy-rich carbohydrates; carrots and celery supply fiber, vitamins, and minerals; broth contributes moisture and minerals. Sodium varies with broth type; to keep sodium lower, use a low-sodium broth and trim visible fat from the beef.

children.name: Shrimp Fried Rice — Citrus Fresh Set
children.short_description: Shrimp fried rice with peas, carrots, egg, and soy sauce—a quick, protein-rich grain dish with vegetables.
children.meal_suggestion: Serve as a balanced plate: about 1 cup cooked rice with 4–6 oz shrimp and a generous portion of peas and carrots. To reduce sodium, use low-sodium soy sauce or tamari; substitute white rice with brown rice for more fiber. Pair with a citrus side to emphasize the Citrus Fresh Set.
children.nutrition_note: Protein-rich from shrimp and egg; carbohydrates from rice; fiber from peas and carrots. Moderate to high sodium from soy sauce; consider low-sodium soy sauce or limiting portion. The Citrus Fresh Set can add vitamin C if paired with a citrus beverage or garnish.

children.name: Shrimp Fried Rice — Garlic-Ginger Bowl
children.short_description: Garlic-ginger shrimp fried rice bowl with peas, carrots, and egg—protein and starch in a quick, savory dish; mindful of sodium and shellfish allergen.
children.meal_suggestion: To boost fiber and reduce sodium: use brown rice or cauliflower rice; choose a low-sodium soy sauce; add more vegetables (peppers, broccoli); reduce oil and use a small amount of sesame oil for flavor.
children.nutrition_note: Protein-rich due to shrimp and egg; includes carbs from rice and fiber from peas and carrots; may be high in sodium from soy sauce; shellfish allergen present; cooking oil adds fat; can be made lower-sodium and higher-fiber with substitutions.
